description: Prisoner store consumption. For every five Prisoner Points in a hex, the capturing player must expend one Store Point per Operations Stage (NOT per Game-Turn). These stores are expended before any other stores may be allocated. The stores need not be physically present; they are subtracted from the nearest supply dump.

cases.rule_clause: For every five Prisoner Points in a hex, the capturing Player must expend one Store Point per Operations Stage (not Game-Turn)

cases.description: 4 prisoner points: below threshold of 5, no stores consumed (4 // 5 = 0)

cases.description: 5 prisoner points, only 0.5 stores available: 1 store owed but only 0.5 present; stores drawn down to 0 (partial draw)
cases.rule_clause: They need not be present; they are subtracted from the nearest supply dump, etc.
cases.note: The rule says stores 'need not be present'; they are subtracted from the nearest supply dump. stores_after is clamped to 0 when the dump cannot cover the full owed amount.

cases.rule_clause: For every five Prisoner Points in a hex, the capturing Player must expend one Store Point per Operations Stage (not Game-Turn)

cases.description: Semantic marker: rule explicitly states per Operations Stage, not per Game-Turn. Two Operations Stages per game-turn means prisoners cost twice per turn. This case documents the correct frequency.
cases.rule_clause: the capturing Player must expend one Store Point per Operations Stage (not Game-Turn). These Stores are expended before any other stores may be allocated.
cases.note: This case illustrates that if there are 2 Operations Stages in a game-turn, the 1-store-per-5-prisoners cost applies twice, for a total of 2 stores consumed over the full game-turn. The 'not Game-Turn' parenthetical in the rule is explicit.
